revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。 == ...'
revision.text:
'''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。
== 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * 田中・舟森山古墳 * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[福島県の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]]

'''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。
== 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * 田中・舟森山古墳 * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[福島県の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]]

'''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。
== 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * 田中・舟森山古墳 * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]]

'''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。古墳時代前期の築造とみられ、同じ会津盆地に造られた一箕古墳群や宇内青津古墳群と共存していたとみられる。
== 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * 田中・舟森山古墳 * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]]

'''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。古墳時代前期の築造とみられ、同じ会津盆地に存在する一箕古墳群や宇内青津古墳群を造った豪族に次ぐ第三の勢力が築造したと共存したとみられる。
== 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * 田中・舟森山古墳 * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]]

Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japa..."

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * **Fukasawa Kofun** * **Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun** * **Jūkyūdan Kofun** * **Kannommori Kofun** == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes

They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four burial mounds located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okunokoyama Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one square-shaped mound (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:kunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] [[en:Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:08 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = 雄国山麓
古墳群 | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|位置}} }} '''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。 == 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。古墳時代前期の築造とみられ、同じ会津盆地に存在する一箕古墳群や宇内青津古墳群を造った豪族に次ぐ第三の勢力が築造したとみられる。 == 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * [[田中・舟森山古墳]] * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳 == 参考文献 == * 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029、新泉社、2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[雄国山麓古墳群]] to [[Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:08 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = 雄国山麓
古墳群 | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|位置}} }} '''雄国山麓古墳群'''（おぐにさんろくこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]にある、4基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。 == 概要 == 方墳1基と前方後方墳3基からなる。古墳時代前期の築造とみられ、同じ会津盆地に存在する一箕古墳群や宇内青津古墳群を造った豪族に次ぐ第三の勢力が築造したとみられる。 == 古墳 == * 深沢古墳 * [[田中・舟森山古墳]] * 十九壇古墳 * 観音森古墳 == 参考文献 == * 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029、新泉社、2006年 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[一箕古墳群]] * [[宇内青津古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくにさんろくこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] [[sn:kunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] [[en:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|version=87493605|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture | label = Oguni Footslopes
Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|version=87493605|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kitakata City]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] [[sn:kunokoyama Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] [[en:Oguni Foothills Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]] [[Category:福島県の古墳]]

The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|version=87493605|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kitakata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q17224644}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Kofun Group | position = top | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Oguni Footslopes Kofun Group''' (雄国山麓古墳群, Oguni-sanroku Kofun-gun) is a group of four Kofuns located in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The group comprises one {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (方墳, hōfun) and three {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. They are believed to have been constructed during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|version=87493605|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q17224644}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kitakata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

They are believed to have been constructed during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun were likely built by a third prominent clan in the Aizu Basin, following the clans that constructed the {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Kofun in the Group == * '''Fukasawa Kofun''' * '''Tanaka Funamori-yama Kofun''' * '''Jūkyūdan Kofun''' * '''Kannommori Kofun''' == References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *"The Origin of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun"* (Series: *Learning from Sites* 029), Shinseisha, 2006 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Ikki Kofun Group|ja|一箕古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Coord|37|36|10.0|N|139|55|06.0|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|version=87493605|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雄国山麓古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q17224644}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kitakata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
